scenarios.description: traverse: replacement in head on a mid-tree node with siblings triggers B8→B11 path and correct depth adjustments
scenarios.precondition: root Element with two children, and visitor replaces the second-level node in head with a new node at same index
scenarios.expectedResult: Traversal continues into replaced node; head/tail called in correct order and depths
pseudocode: TEST TC13 traverse replacement on deep sibling
public void tail(Node n,int d) { log.add("t:"+n.nodeName()+":"+d); }
// 'c' appears in log as replacement for 'b', and depth returns properly

scenarios.description: traverse: removal in head on a leaf with no nextSibling triggers B9 removal→ ascend→ continue at parent
scenarios.precondition: root Element with single child and visitor removes child in head
scenarios.expectedResult: Child is removed, tail never called on it, traversal ascends back to root, then terminates
pseudocode: TEST TC14 traverse removal without siblings
public void tail(Node n,int d) { calls.add("t:"+n.nodeName()+":"+d); }

scenarios.description: traverse: ascend-loop branch when a node has nextSibling!=null at depth=0 triggers B16→B20→B22
scenarios.precondition: root Element with two leaf children, visitor only logs, assertions enabled
scenarios.expectedResult: Traverses first child (has nextSibling, depth=1) through B18, but for root-level nextSibling at depth=0 goes B16→B20→B22
pseudocode: TEST TC15 traverse sibling ascend at depth zero
public void head(Node n,int d) { seq.add("h:"+n.nodeName()+":"+d); }
public void tail(Node n,int d) { seq.add("t:"+n.nodeName()+":"+d); }
// ensure 'b' is visited via B22->B23, depth remains correct
